title: Selenicereus macdonaldiae
taxonomy: Selenicereus macdonaldiae is a species of flowering cactus in the genus Selenicereus, which is a group of epiphytic, vining cacti. Selenicereus macdonaldiae belongs to the family Cactaceae and is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. It is classified as a type of climbing cactus, with stems that can grow up to several meters in length. The species is a member of the tribe Hylocereeae, which is a subfamily of cacti known for their showy flowers. Selenicereus macdonaldiae is also known for its white, funnel-shaped flowers that bloom at night and last for only one day.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
